# Transcrição: 3_ways_to_improve_impromptu_speaking

- **Fonte:** 3_ways_to_improve_impromptu_speaking
- **Duração:** 01:07
- **Idioma:** en
- **Modelo:** small

---

## [00:00 - 01:07]

There are three reasons as to why one would not be good at impromptu speeches. Number one, we don't know what the person is actually asking us. Number two, we don't actually know the answer to the question. Number three, we don't actually feel confident in our ability to speak and therefore get a mental blank because we're feeling nervous. If you don't know what the person's asking you or you don't understand the question, the first thing you must do is seek further clarity. Give me a few moments. I really want to think of an answer that's going to really help you. The fact that you're waiting and pausing and thinking is actually more meaningful and more valuable for the person asking the question. The second reason, if you actually don't know the answer to the question, the three most powerful words you can say is, I don't know. And finally, a lot of the times we tend to go blank in our minds when we're speaking to strangers and they ask us questions is because we're not feeling confident. We don't have self-confidence. We don't have high self-esteem. So what you have to do to remedy this is you have to go out there and meet as many new strangers as you can. That's the only real way for you to build confidence in this space.
